entities.name: PEANUT
entities.description: Arachis hypogaea L., the crop studied for its response to soil temperature

entities.name: SOIL TEMPERATURE
entities.description: The environmental temperature of the soil, specifically day/night regimes of 20/14, 26/20, 32/26, and 38/32 C

entities.name: VEGETATIVE GROWTH
entities.description: The growth phase of the plant prior to reproduction

entities.name: REPRODUCTIVE GROWTH
entities.description: The growth phase of the plant involving flower and pod development

entities.name: DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: The overall process of growth and maturation of the peanut plant

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The total agricultural output of the peanut crop

entities.name: AGRONOMIC MANAGEMENT
entities.description: The practice of managing crops to improve yield

entities.name: BREEDING
entities.description: The process of selecting and developing improved crop genotypes

entities.name: GREENHOUSE
entities.description: The controlled environment where the study was conducted

entities.name: SPANISH GENOTYPES
entities.description: Three specific genetic variations of peanut used in the investigation

entities.name: PEG PENETRATION
entities.description: The developmental stage when the peg enters the soil

entities.name: MATURITY
entities.description: The final stage of plant development

relationships.description: location where temperature regimes were imposed
